checks.description: Every truncated case was expanded before semantic judgment
checks.detail: 4 cases required full-evidence expansion: B01-002 (13 omitted → 13 retrieved), B01-003 (144 omitted → 50 retrieved; mandatory), B01-006 (18 omitted → 18 retrieved), B01-009 (30 omitted → 30 retrieved). All 4 performed expansion before final semantic judgment. Expansion changed interpretation for B01-002, B01-003, and B01-009.

B01-006 record contains: full_evidence_expansion_performed: true, eligible_user_segments_total: 38, segments_in_bundle: 20, segments_retrieved_in_expansion: 18, 18 Stage 3R references listed. All required expansion fields are present. Verified in quality_review.md B01-006 section. Recovery audit also documents B01-006 expansion correctly.

All 9 non-empty files parse without errors. candidate_entities.jsonl: 6 records. candidate_relationships.jsonl: 3 records. candidate_evidence.jsonl: 16 records. excluded_interactions.jsonl: 8 records. missing_source_impacts.jsonl: 4 records. unresolved_items.jsonl: 0 records (empty). rule_application_log.jsonl: 20 records. validation_report.json: valid JSON. completion_manifest.json: to be verified after creation.

Verified: candidate_entities.jsonl (6 unique IDs), candidate_relationships.jsonl (3 unique IDs), candidate_evidence.jsonl (16 unique IDs), excluded_interactions.jsonl (8 unique IDs), missing_source_impacts.jsonl (4 unique IDs), rule_application_log.jsonl (20 unique IDs). No duplicates found.

Null filenames are preserved as null in missing_source_impacts.jsonl for all three missing-source sets. affected_attachment_occurrence_ids is empty array where specific occurrence IDs are unavailable. No attachment occurrence IDs are referenced without resolution.

4 MSI records (b01c1:msi:001 through b01c1:msi:004) are created and resolve. Missing source sub-IDs (b01c1:ms:001 through b01c1:ms:012) are assigned within MSI records and consistently referenced. No missing-source ID is referenced without a corresponding MSI record.

checks.description: Applied rule IDs exist in the 23-rule frozen rule set
checks.detail: 13 unique rule IDs applied across this checkpoint: hr-002, hr-003, hr-007, hr-009, hr-012, hr-013, hr-014, hr-016, hr-017, hr-018, hr-019, hr-020, hr-021. All 13 exist in frozen_decision_rules.json (rule_count: 23, including hr-001 through hr-022 plus hr-008a). No non-existent rule IDs were applied.

The only explicit_acceptance endorsement in this checkpoint is in CAND-B01C1-006 (evidence record b01c1:ev:014: user's work authorization opt-out instruction in segment e64d28518c2eccff994d9b85e77cff7c). This is a direct user instruction with user-origin evidence. All other endorsements are implicit_continuation or no_endorsement, which do not require explicit user-origin evidence.

All null filenames in missing_source_impacts.jsonl are recorded as null. b01c1:msi:001 filename: null. b01c1:msi:002 filename: null. b01c1:msi:003 filename: null. b01c1:msi:004 filename: null. filename_invented: false for all records.
filename_invented: false for all 4 MSI records. No filenames were constructed or inferred from context. hr-018 applied across all attachment-bearing cases (B01-003, B01-007, B01-009).

checks.description: Duplicate attachment occurrences did not inflate evidence
checks.detail: Three null-filename attachments in B01-007 are treated as three separate source material occurrences (one each from Naomi's Group Log templates), not as one attachment counted three times. No duplicate occurrence inflation detected. hr-018 applied.

All 4 MSI records document missing sources with do_not_infer semantics. hr-017 applied in all relevant cases. No content was reconstructed from unavailable binary sources. Extracted text is used as evidence only where it was available through Stage 3R.

review_classification values used: 'covered_by_existing_rule' (5 candidates) and 'conditional_pass' is not a review_classification value — b01c1:rule:012 has conditional_pass in validation_status only, not in candidate review_classification. CAND-B01C1-005 uses review_classification: 'covered_by_existing_rule' which is valid. All review_classification values are from the allowed enum: [covered_by_existing_rule, new_reusable_rule, case_specific_exception, genuinely_unresolved, null].

checks.description: Covered-by-rule items are absent from the human-review queue
checks.detail: All 6 candidates have requires_human_review: false. All 20 rule application records have prevented_human_review: true. No items requiring human review exist in this checkpoint. One rule application (b01c1:rule:012) is tagged validation_status: conditional_pass with documented condition, but this does not trigger human review — it is a documented borderline case resolved by rule.

No files were modified in context_pipeline/framework_freeze/. All frozen framework files (frozen_policy.json, frozen_decision_rules.json, frozen_confirmed_resolutions.json, review_thresholds.json, schemas) were read-only during this recovery operation.

No Stage 3R, Stage 3R-P, Stage 4, or Stage 5A files were modified. selected_case_ids.json, case_extractions.jsonl, recovery_audit.json, and recovery_audit.md were not modified (no defects requiring correction were found).
